description: Candidate deflects by attributing the decision to their team or manager rather than engaging with the technical question themselves — classic responsibility-disclaiming deflection.

judge_input.active_question_text: You're inheriting a portfolio of ~40-50 integrations across cloud and on-prem. In your first 90 days, how would you assess, stabilize, and standardize the estate?
active_question_positive_evidence: Creates an inventory and dependency map
active_question_red_flags: Vague stakeholder meetings with no technical assessment

active_question_rubric.excellent: Concrete 90-day plan with inventory, metric baselines, prioritized remediation.
active_question_rubric.meets_bar: Plausible plan covering assessment and some metrics.

active_question_signal_metadata.value: 8+ years overall experience in enterprise integration design and implementation

active_question_remaining_probes.0: What evidence and environment data would you gather in week 1?
active_question_remaining_probes.1: How would you prioritize remediation work?

8+ years overall experience in enterpris.signal_value: 8+ years overall experience in enterprise integration design and implementation

recent_turns.text: You're inheriting a portfolio of integrations. In your first 90 days, how would you assess and stabilize the estate?

judge_input.candidate_utterance: That would really depend on what my manager and the team decide. I'd just follow whatever process they already have in place and support the existing workflows. I'm flexible and adapt to team norms.
